summary: Nigerian poet. Known for the collection Heavensgate and the poem 'Elegy for Alto.' Died fighting for Biafra in the Nigerian Civil War. Poetry addresses the Igbo deity Idoto.

works.name: Heavensgate
works.description: Poetry collection opening with address to mother Idoto at the oilbean tree.

clues.clue: Opens 'Before you, mother Idoto, / naked I stand'; speaker leans on an oilbean tree waiting for Igbo river deity
examples: said 'Before you, mother Idoto, / naked I stand' to open his collection Heavensgate

clues.clue: 'The Passage' — speaker waits for Idoto personified as a river
examples: waiting for an Igbo deity personified as a river in the poem 'The Passage'

clues.clue: Ends with 'The new star appears, foreshadows... a going and coming that goes on forever'; designated '(with drum accompaniment)'
examples: designates that it is meant to be read '(with drum accompaniment)'

clues.clue: Died fighting for Biafra in the Nigerian Civil War
examples: this Nigerian poet who died fighting for Biafra

clues.clue: 'Hurrah for Thunder' — 'if I don't learn to shut my mouth I'll soon go to hell'
examples: 'if I don't learn to shut my mouth I'll soon go to hell'

comprehensive_summary: Christopher Okigbo (1932-1967) was a Nigerian poet who died fighting for Biafra in the Nigerian Civil War. His collection Heavensgate opens with 'Before you, mother Idoto, / naked I stand,' addressing the Igbo river deity. 'The Passage' describes the speaker leaning on an oilbean tree waiting for Idoto. 'Elegy for Alto' includes the instruction '(with drum accompaniment).' 'Hurrah for Thunder' contains the line 'if I don't learn to shut my mouth I'll soon go to hell.'

cards.front: Poem: opens 'Before you, mother Idoto, / naked I stand'
Poem: speaker leans on an oilbean tree waiting for an Igbo river deity
Poet: Nigerian poet who died fighting for Biafra in the Nigerian Civil War
